title: Implement workflow trigger, test gate, and build artifact scaffold
description: Create `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with the push-to-main trigger, the `test` job, and the baseline `build` job structure. Include Python/uv setup, dependency installation, the required pytest invocation, `needs:` ordering into `build`, artifact upload for `dist/`, and any job outputs needed by downstream publish steps, but do not yet add the version-bump commit/tag/release automation.
acceptance_criteria: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` exists and triggers on pushes to `main`.
The `test` job installs dependencies with `uv sync --all-extras` and runs `uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q`.
The `build` job depends on `test`, installs the package build environment, runs `uv build`, and uploads `dist/` as an artifact named `dist`.
The workflow structure leaves clear outputs or step placeholders for downstream version/release automation without mixing in unrelated changes.

handoff_summary.completed: Created and committed `.github/workflows/ci.yml` satisfying all four acceptance criteria.
handoff_summary.remaining: N/A — downstream version-bump, tag, and release automation is out of scope for this bead and reserved for a subsequent bead.
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. The workflow file is self-contained and does not overlap with any other in-progress bead's file scope.
handoff_summary.next_action: A follow-up bead should add the version-bump commit, git tag, and GitHub Release steps to the `build` job (or a new `publish` job), using the `dist` artifact uploaded here and the `artifact_name` job output as the handoff point.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` was the only file in scope and no sibling beads claim it.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used `astral-sh/setup-uv@v4` (the official uv GitHub Action) rather than a pip-based install to keep the setup idiomatic and fast. Both jobs install via `uv sync --all-extras` to ensure dev dependencies (pytest, pytest-xdist) are present in both environments — this is slightly redundant in `build` but makes the jobs independent and avoids caching complexity. The `build` job's `outputs` block contains `artifact_name: dist` as a static value; downstream jobs can reference `needs.build.outputs.artifact_name` to locate the uploaded artifact without hard-coding the string twice.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: The workflow itself is YAML configuration, not executable Python. The tester agent should verify: (1) the YAML is valid and parses correctly, (2) the trigger fires only on `main` pushes, (3) job dependency ordering (`needs: test`) is correct, (4) artifact name matches what a downstream publish job would reference. A real integration test would require a GitHub Actions run, which is outside the automated test suite.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Version-bump, git tagging, and GitHub Release steps are intentionally absent — reserved for the downstream bead that handles release automation. The `outputs.artifact_name` field is a static string, not a computed value; a future bead may want to derive it dynamically (e.g., from the package version). The workflow does not yet cache uv or the virtual environment, which could slow CI runs on large dependency sets.

conflict_risks: None. `.github/workflows/ci.yml` was the only file in scope and no sibling beads claim it.

execution_history.summary: Created `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with push-to-main trigger, `test` job (Python 3.11 + uv setup, `uv sync --all-extras`, `uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q`), and `build` job (`needs: test`, `uv build`, `dist/` artifact upload via `actions/upload-artifact@v4`). Job outputs field left as a placeholder for downstream publish/release automation.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.

The `test` job installs dependencies with `uv sync --all-extras` and runs `uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q`.

tool_input.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  test:
    name: Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: uv sync --all-extras

      - name: Run tests
        run: uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q

  build:
    name: Build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: test
    outputs:
      # Placeholder output for downstream version/release automation
      artifact_name: dist
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4

      - name: Install build environment
        run: uv sync --all-extras

      - name: Build package
        run: uv build

      - name: Upload dist artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: dist/
          if-no-files-found: error
